title: श्री रघुनाथाष्टकम्
devanagariText:
वैदेहीसहितं सुरद्रुमतले हैमे महामण्डपे
मध्ये पुष्पकमासने मणिमये वीरासनेऽसंस्थितम्।
अग्रे वाचयति प्रभञ्जनसुते तत्त्वं मुनिभ्यः परं
व्याख्यान्तं भरतादिभिः परिवृतं रामं भजे श्यामलम्॥१॥

क्षीरोदन्वदुदन्वदम्बुजगतं चन्द्रानने कम्बुजं
मन्दारस्रजमादधानमुरसि स्रग्भिस्तमालद्युतिम्।
बिभ्राणं सरसीरुहोदरसमं रामं रमानायकं
भव्यं भूमिसुताविरञ्चिपरमं रामं भजे श्यामलम्॥२॥

शान्तं शाश्वतमप्रमेयमनघं निर्वाणशान्तिप्रदं
ब्रह्मशम्भुफणीन्द्रसेव्यमनिशं वेदान्तवेद्यं विभुम्।
रामाख्यं जगदीश्वरं सुरगुरुं मायामनुष्यं हरिं
वन्देऽहं करुणाकरं रघुवरं भूपालचूडामणिम्॥३॥

राजीवायतलोचनं रणविधौ दीक्षागुरुं लक्ष्मणं
सीतामुक्तिसमर्पणेन कृतिनं विश्वप्रियं राघवम्।
भक्तानुग्रहकारिणं रघुपतिं साकेतपुण्यालयं
वन्देऽहं करुणाकरं रघुवरं भूपालचूडामणिम्॥४॥

यो ददाति शरणागतेषु सदयो विश्वासपूर्णां गतिं
राक्षसेष्वपि यो महानुभवतो दत्तेऽभयं वालिने।
यः कार्ये कठिने विभीषणमपि स्नेहेन रक्षत्यसौ
वन्देऽहं करुणाकरं रघुवरं भूपालचूडामणिम्॥५॥

मन्दारं बहुयोजनायतमहापुष्पं सुगन्धान्वितं
गन्धर्वैर्मधुरं ततेन विबुधैः सम्पूजितं सर्वदा।
यत्पादाम्बुजमाश्रितं भवभयभ्रान्तेर्विमुक्तिप्रदं
वन्देऽहं करुणाकरं रघुवरं भूपालचूडामणिम्॥६॥

यस्य प्राणसमां जनैर्जनकजां वैदेहसुतां सतीं
तल्लोकत्रयशोभनं मणिमयं सिंहासनं शाश्वतम्।
साकेतं च पुरीमयोध्यमखिलां गोप्तुं समर्थोऽभवत्
वन्देऽहं करुणाकरं रघुवरं भूपालचूडामणिम्॥७॥

कोदण्डं कनकाङ्गदं कनककं चापं शरं सायकं
हेमाम्भोजदलायतायतदृशं पीताम्बरालंकृतम्।
सीतालक्ष्मणसंयुतं जटिलतं जाम्बूनदाभं रमं
वन्देऽहं करुणाकरं रघुवरं भूपालचूडामणिम्॥८॥

रघुनाथाष्टकमिदं यः पठेत् सततं नरः।
राघवस्य प्रसादेन सर्वसिद्धिमवाप्नुयात्॥

॥ इति श्री रघुनाथाष्टकं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
Vaidehisahitam Suradruma-Tale Haime Mahamandape
Madhye Pushpakam-Asane Manimaye Virasane'samsthitam.
Agre Vachayati Prabhanjanasute Tattvam Munibhyah Param
Vyakhyantam Bharatadibhih Parivritam Ramam Bhaje Shyamalam.||1||

Kshirodanvadudanvad-Ambujaga-tam Chandranane Kambujam
Mandarasrajam Adadhanam Urasi Sragbhis Tamala-Dyutim.
Bibhranam Sarasiruhodara-Samam Ramam Ramanayakam
Bhavyam Bhumisuta-Viranchi-Paramam Ramam Bhaje Shyamalam.||2||

Shantam Shashvatam Aprameyam Anagham Nirvanashanti-Pradam
Brahma-Shambhu-Phanindra-Sevyam Anisham Vedanta-Vedyam Vibhum.
Ramakhyam Jagadishvaram Suragurum Mayamanushyam Harim
Vande'ham Karunaakaram Raghuvaram Bhupala-Chudamanim.||3||

Rajivayata-Lochanam Ranavidhau Diksha-Gurum Lakshmanam
Sita-Mukti-Samarpanena Kritinam Vishvapriyam Raghavam.
Bhaktanugraha-Karinam Raghupatim Saketa-Punyalayam
Vande'ham Karunakaram Raghuvaram Bhupala-Chudamanim.||4||

Yo Dadati Sharanaga-teshu Sadayo Vishvasapurnam Gatim
Rakshase-shvapi Yo Mahanubhavato Datte'bhayam Valine.
Yah Karye Kathine Vibhishanam-Api Snehena Rakshaty-Asau
Vande'ham Karunakaram Raghuvaram Bhupala-Chudamanim.||5||

Mandaram Bahu-Yojanayata-Mahapushpam Sugandhan-vitam
Gandharvairmdhuram Tatena Vibudhaih Sampujitam Sarvada.
Yatpadambujam Ashritam Bhavabhaya-Bhranter Vimuktipradam
Vande'ham Karunakaram Raghuvaram Bhupala-Chudamanim.||6||

Yasya Pranasamam Janair Janakajam Vaidehasutam Satim
Tallokatra-yashobhanam Manimayam Simhasanam Shashvatam.
Saketam Cha Purim Ayodhyam Akhilam Goptum Samartho'bhavat
Vande'ham Karunakaram Raghuvaram Bhupala-Chudamanim.||7||

Kodandam Kanakangadam Kanakakam Chapam Sharam Sayakam
Hemambhoja-Dalayata-yata-Drisham Pitambara-Lankritam.
Sita-Lakshmana-Samyutam Jatilatam Jambunadabham Ramam
Vande'ham Karunakaram Raghuvaram Bhupala-Chudamanim.||8||

Raghunatha-Ashtakam-Idam Yah Pathet Satatam Narah.
Raghavasya Prasadena Sarvasiddhim Avapnuyat.

|| Iti Shri Raghunatha Ashtakam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
वैदेही (सीता) सहित, कल्पवृक्ष तले, हेम महामण्डप में, पुष्पक मणिमय सिंहासन पर वीरासन में विराजमान, समक्ष पवनसुत (हनुमान) को सुनाते, मुनियों को परम तत्त्व का व्याख्यान करते, भरत आदि से परिवृत श्यामल राम को मैं भजता हूँ।

क्षीरसागर सा कमलयुक्त, चन्द्रमुख, कम्बु (शंख) सा कण्ठ, मन्दार माला उरस्थल पर, तमाल सी द्युति — रमानायक, भूमिसुता-विरञ्चि-परम राम को मैं भजता हूँ।

शान्त, शाश्वत, अप्रमेय, अनघ, निर्वाणशान्तिप्रद, ब्रह्मा-शम्भु-शेषनाग द्वारा सेव्य, वेदान्तवेद्य — मायामनुष्य हरि, करुणाकर रघुवर भूपालचूडामणि की मैं वन्दना करता हूँ।

कमलायत लोचन, रण में दीक्षागुरु लक्ष्मण, सीता की मुक्ति के समर्पण से कृतार्थ — भक्तानुग्रहकारी रघुपति, साकेतपुण्यालय रघुवर की वन्दना।

जो शरणागतों को सदय विश्वासपूर्ण गति देते हैं, राक्षसों में भी महानुभाव विभीषण को स्नेह से रक्षते हैं — करुणाकर रघुवर की वन्दना।

जिनके पादकमल आश्रित भव-भय-भ्रान्ति से मुक्ति प्रदान करते हैं — करुणाकर रघुवर भूपालचूडामणि की वन्दना।

जिनकी प्राणसमान जनकजा सती सीता, त्रिलोकशोभन मणिमय शाश्वत सिंहासन, सम्पूर्ण अयोध्या की गोप्ता — रघुवर की वन्दना।

कोदण्डधारी, कनकाङ्गद, स्वर्णकमल सदृश विशाल नेत्र, पीताम्बरालंकृत, सीता-लक्ष्मण संयुत, जटिल, जाम्बूनद (सुवर्ण) आभायुक्त — करुणाकर रघुवर की वन्दना।

जो मनुष्य सतत रघुनाथाष्टक पढ़ता है, राघव के प्रसाद से सर्वसिद्धि प्राप्त करता है।

viniyog.shloka: अस्य श्रीरघुनाथाष्टकमन्त्रस्य वाल्मीकि ऋषिः। शार्दूलविक्रीडित छन्दः। श्रीरघुनाथ देवता। श्रीं रामाय नमः बीजम्। करुणाशक्तिः। सर्वसिद्धिप्राप्तिः कीलकम्। श्रीरामप्रीत्यर्थे पाठे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Exquisite eight-verse prayer in the Shardula-Vikridita metre
Each verse ends with the powerful refrain praising Rama as Bhupala Chudamani
Invokes sarva siddhi through raghava's grace
Ideal for those who seek refuge and surrender to Lord Rama
Powerful for Ram Navami and daily Rama devotion
description: Raghunatha Ashtakam (श्री रघुनाथाष्टकम्) is an eight-verse devotional hymn dedicated to Lord Rama. This revered text originates from Traditional Sanskrit Stotra. Regular recitation is believed to invoke Rama's supreme compassion (Karunakara), grant Sarva Siddhi through Raghava's grace, and exquisite eight-verse prayer in the Shardula-Vikridita metre. This sacred text is ideally recited on Sunday (Ravivar) and during Ram Navami, Dussehra, and Vivah Panchami for enhanced blessings. With 8 verses and a reading time of about 6 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Raghunatha Ashtakam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Raghunatha Ashtakam in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Eight majestic verses praising Lord Rama as the crown jewel of kings for grace and all success.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Raghunatha Ashtakam and what makes its structure distinctive?
seoFaqs.answer: The Raghunatha Ashtakam is an eight-verse Sanskrit stotra in praise of Lord Rama, addressed throughout as Raghunatha — the Lord of the Raghu dynasty — and as Bhupala Chudamani, the crown jewel of kings. The hymn is composed in the elegant Shardula-Vikridita metre, the same long-syllabic chhandas used in many Adi Shankaracharya compositions, and each verse closes with the refrain Vande'ham Karunakaram Raghuvaram Bhupala-Chudamanim, anchoring the recitation in surrender to Rama's compassion. The opening verse paints a darbar tableau of Shyama Rama seated in Pushpaka with Sita, Hanuman before him reciting the Tattva, and Bharata and brothers in attendance. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi; Sanskritdocuments.org Raghunatha Ashtakam.)

seoFaqs.question: Where is the Raghunatha Ashtakam preserved and what is its date?
seoFaqs.answer: The Raghunatha Ashtakam is preserved in the classical Sanskrit stotra tradition and circulates widely in Rama-bhakti compendia such as Stotra Ratnavali, Stotra Mala, and the digital archives of Sanskritdocuments.org and Stotra Nidhi. The oldest manuscript attributions vary — some sources place it within the Padma Purana stotra cycle, others within the Vaishnava devotional output of the medieval period. The composition's polished Shardula-Vikridita metre and the iconographic depiction of Rama with Hanuman expounding Tattva to munis suggests an Adhyatma-Ramayana milieu, but in the absence of a single firmly attested author it is conventionally listed as preserved in classical tradition. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi; Hindupedia Rama stotras.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the significance of the refrain Bhupala Chudamani in the stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: Bhupala Chudamani (literally crown-jewel of kings) is the closing epithet of every verse and the spiritual signature of the entire ashtakam. Rama is praised not merely as an incarnation but as the ideal raja-purusha who embodies dharma-rakshana, sharanagata-vatsalya (love for those who take refuge), and karuna (compassion). The refrain frames each verse with the salutation Vande'ham Karunakaram — I bow to the compassionate one — establishing that Rama's kingship is constituted by mercy rather than mere sovereignty. The phalashruti accordingly promises sarva-siddhi to the devotee who recites with this consciousness of Rama as the king of compassion. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi commentary; Hindupedia Rama epithets.)

seoFaqs.question: What stories does the Raghunatha Ashtakam reference in its verses?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 5 explicitly recalls three episodes that demonstrate Rama's sharanagata-vatsalya: granting refuge to all who surrender (Yo dadati sharanagateshu), bestowing abhaya (fearlessness) on Vali (datte abhayam Valine), and protecting Vibhishana with affection in the difficult moment of his defection from Lanka (Vibhishanam api snehena rakshati). Verse 7 evokes Rama as the protector of Sita, the manimaya simhasana of Saketa, and the entirety of Ayodhya. Verse 8 paints him with kodanda bow, kanaka-angada ornaments, lotus-petal eyes, golden complexion (Jambunada), Sita and Lakshmana at his side, and matted locks (jatila) — capturing the vana-vasi yet royal Rama. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi verse-by-verse; Sanskritdocuments.org.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Raghunatha Ashtakam be chanted for best results?
seoFaqs.answer: Sunday (Ravivara) is the prescribed weekday for Rama recitations as it is dedicated to Surya, the kula-devata of the Suryavamsa Raghu lineage. The ashtakam is especially recited during Ram Navami (Chaitra Shukla Navami, Rama's appearance day), Vivah Panchami (Margashirsha Shukla Panchami, the wedding of Rama and Sita), Dussehra/Vijayadashami (commemorating Ravana's defeat), and during the morning brahma-muhurta sandhya for daily Rama upasana. Chanting eleven, twenty-one or one hundred and eight times is standard; with only eight verses and a six-minute reading time, multiple cycles fit easily within a half-hour parayana session. (Sources: Drikpanchang Ram Navami; Stotra Nidhi vidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the phalashruti of the Raghunatha Ashtakam promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The closing phalashruti declares Raghunatha-ashtakam-idam yah pathet satatam narah, Raghavasya prasadena sarva-siddhim avapnuyat — the devotee who continually recites this ashtakam attains all siddhis through Raghava's grace. Sarva-siddhi in the Rama-bhakti tradition is understood as the four purusharthas (dharma, artha, kama, moksha) along with the eight classical siddhis. Devotees report particular benefit in matters requiring courage and dharmic clarity — career decisions, household leadership, protection from adharma — which align with Rama's kingly archetype. The ashtakam is also widely recited for protection during travel, family well-being, and progeny since Rama is the kula-devata of countless North Indian Hindu families. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi phalashruti; Hindupedia Rama bhakti.)

seoFaqs.question: How does the Raghunatha Ashtakam differ from Rama Ashtakam and Rama Raksha Stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: All three are short Rama hymns but serve different functions. The Raghunatha Ashtakam (eight Shardula-Vikridita verses) emphasises Rama's royal compassion and ends every verse with Bhupala Chudamani, suited for aishvarya-bhakti and contemplation of Rama-rajya. The Rama Ashtakam (eight verses, varied metres, attributed to Mukunda-mala-stotra tradition) glorifies Rama as advayam and grants moksha as its phalashruti. The Rama Raksha Stotra by Budhakaushika Rishi is a longer thirty-eight verse kavach-style protective armour invoking Rama to guard each limb. The Raghunatha Ashtakam is best for Sunday parayana, the Rama Ashtakam for liberation-focused practice, and the Rama Raksha for protection. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi; Hindupedia Rama stotras.)
